artifact_title: Failure Attribution Note: Fluent Artifact Contradicted by Trace Evidence

claims.text: The failure attribution correctly uses the defined taxonomy by assigning the root cause to one of the specified layers (scorer).
The presence of preserved raw trace allowed manual detection of the conflict between the fluent artifact and the actual request/response.
Scorer faults are known to occur, as prior contamination scoring misclassifications demonstrate, making the scorer a plausible failure point.

conclusion: The failure is attributed to the scorer layer due to plausible misclassification of a fluent but unfaithful model output.
declared_losses: Undetected autonomy risk due to scoring oversight
recommended_action: Audit scorer heuristics and consider integrating raw trace comparison to detect mismatches.
